scenario.title: Draw the flow — tag-filter navigation
scenario.question: How does a tag-filter navigation actually flow, end to end?
scenario.lede: One drawing, four named paths. Emphasize a path to trace it; the same flow is written out in words below, so nothing lives only in the picture.

flow.ariaLabel: Tag-filter navigation flow with normal, decision, failure, and recovery paths
flow.caption: Every navigation walks left to right; the branch at “tag known?” separates the normal render from the empty-state path, and “clear filter” is the recovery route back to the full catalog.

pathViews.label: Normal path
steps: A navigation (or back/forward) carries ?tag= in the URL
parseTagFromSearch returns the tag, or null when absent
The tag matches at least one skill, so the branch takes “known”
replaceChildren swaps in exactly the matching rows

pathViews.label: The decision
steps: The single branch in the flow: does any skill carry the requested tag?
“Known” continues to the normal render; there is no partial match
“Unknown” is not an error — it routes to the accepted empty state
A null tag skips the branch entirely and renders the full catalog

pathViews.label: Failure path
steps: The requested tag matches no skill (a stale or mistyped link)
filterByTag would return [], so the flow never reaches it
The empty state renders: it names the miss and shows the real tag set
Nothing is silently swallowed — the URL still shows what was asked

pathViews.label: Recovery path
steps: From the empty state, “clear filter” is the one-click exit
It navigates to the bare URL (no ?tag=)
The same render path runs with a null tag
The full five-skill catalog renders — orientation restored

options.label: The flow is accurate
options.consequence: This drawing and its textual equivalent become the recorded shared understanding of the navigation.

options.label: Needs correction
options.consequence: Name what is wrong or missing in the rationale; the flow is redrawn before anything is recorded.

options.label: Wrong question
options.consequence: The flow answers a different question than the one that matters; scope is restated before redrawing.

recommendation.reason: Every node and edge was walked against src/app.mjs at the recorded revision.

unresolved: whether chip-click interception (slice 5 open question) changes the recovery path

accepted.acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — flow verified against src/app.mjs and confirmed via pasted candidate
accepted.summary: The end-to-end navigation flow — normal render, the single known/unknown branch, the empty-state failure path, and the clear-filter recovery — verified as the shared understanding at a8b0837.

decisions.rationale: Walked each edge against the glue code; the branch and both exits match.

exampleWorking.rationale: Walked each edge against the glue code; the branch and both exits match.

accepted.canonicalBody:
# Tag-filter navigation flow — verified

navigation → parse → (tag known?) → filter → rows, with unknown tags
routing to the accepted empty state and clear-filter recovering to the
full catalog. Textual equivalent recorded alongside the drawing.
